title: austrobaileyaceae
intro: The Austrobaileyaceae is a family of flowering plants that belongs to the flowering plant clade, Angiosperms. It is a distinct group of plants characterized by the presence of small to medium-sized trees and shrubs, often with distinctive seed and fruit structures. This family is primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world, with a significant presence in Australia and surrounding islands. The Austrobaileyaceae family is thought to be one of the most ancient lineages of flowering plants, with fossil records dating back to the Early Cretaceous period.
